tool_name: Restaurant Cold Chain Accept/Reject Cost Calculator
scope: Restaurant Cold Chain Accept/Reject Cost Calculator supports one operation: food_service_margin_and_safety_screening. It is limited to decision-support screening using user-supplied, source-verified inputs.

decision_context.system_boundary: Batch/serving volume, time-temperature exposure, labor/energy cost, spoilage probability, and gross margin.
decision_context.single_operation_scope: Evaluate Restaurant Cold Chain Accept/Reject Cost Calculator as a single decision-support operation using normalized inputs, evidence checks, uncertainty, and business impact.
decision_context.decision_owner: Restaurant owner/operator, chef, purchasing, operations lead, food quality team
decision_context.decision_after_output: serve, hold, discard, adjust labor/menu/cold-chain plan, or escalate for food safety review
decision_context.cost_of_wrong_decision: Food safety, energy, scrap, labor, and menu margin are misread in daily operations.
decision_context.failure_mode_if_formula_wrong: Wrong unit normalization, missing evidence, hidden default, threshold misuse, or formula leak can produce unsafe release, underpriced work, rejected documentation, or avoidable loss.
decision_context.primary_metric: gross margin, food safety margin, and operating cost exposure
required_source_documents: job/ERP record when commercial values are used
owner-approved engineering assumption when no universal reference exists
field_use_limitations: Use for screening and decision support only.
Do not bypass review for REVIEW, HOLD, REJECT, or BLOCKED status.
Verify all standards-sensitive values from approved source documents.

irreversible_commitment_metric: release of food batch, menu price, cold-chain acceptance, labor plan, or service operation

reference_status.public_note: This schema uses standards context only. Exact clauses, tables, coefficients, and acceptance values must be supplied and verified by the user where applicable.

calculation_basis.engineering_engine_summary: Operating model that combines heat-time, labor, product safety, energy, scrap, and margin impact.
calculation_basis.buyer_problem_solved: Connects daily operating decisions with real margin, scrap, energy, product safety, and labor impact.
calculation_basis.red_team_note: Unit-safe schema, physical hard bounds, semantic warnings, hidden-risk explanation, proof pack, and audit seal are mandatory.

inputs.name: Batch Quantity

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Batch Quantity cannot be below the physical lower bound for this calculation context.
physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: Batch Quantity exceeds the configured physical hard bound; verify source evidence before execution.

engineering_range.source: Tool-specific engineering range must be configured from user-verified source evidence or internal approved policy.

reference_values.source: User-verified source document, calibrated measurement, ERP/job record, or approved engineering note.
reference_values.public_note: Reference values are advisory and must not autofill or override measured values.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Attach or confirm source evidence before using this value for a decision.

inputs.user_help_text: Enter the source-verified value for Batch Quantity. The client shows only unit-normalized preview; calculation is server-side.
inputs.warning_if_missing_or_estimated: Batch Quantity is decision-sensitive. Missing or estimated values may move the result to REVIEW or BLOCKED.

rules.suggested_action: Enter the missing value or confirm no calculation should be run.

rules.condition_description: A unit-selectable input has no selected unit.
rules.why_it_matters: Unit ambiguity can create wrong engineering outputs.
rules.suggested_action: Select the display unit before execution.

rules.condition_description: Selected unit is absent from the conversion registry.
rules.why_it_matters: Unsupported conversion can corrupt normalized inputs.

rules.condition_description: Input is NaN, Infinity, or non-numeric.
rules.semantic_message: Input must be a finite number.
Output is non-finite and cannot be displayed.
rules.why_it_matters: Non-finite output indicates invalid formula path or input.
rules.suggested_action: Block public response and inspect server trace.

rules.semantic_message: Value violates a physical hard bound.
rules.why_it_matters: Impossible or unsafe values must fail closed.

rules.condition_description: Input is below advisory engineering range.
rules.semantic_message: Value is below the advisory engineering range.
rules.why_it_matters: Unusual values may indicate wrong units or source data.

rules.condition_description: Input is above advisory engineering range.
rules.semantic_message: Value is above the advisory engineering range.
rules.why_it_matters: Unusual values may indicate wrong units or source data.
The result may not be defensible without evidence.

rules.condition_description: Critical input uses a hidden default.
rules.semantic_message: Hidden defaults are forbidden for critical inputs.

rules.condition_description: Unverified smart default used as critical final value.
rules.semantic_message: Unverified default cannot be final critical input.

rules.condition_description: Formula uses raw input ID instead of normalized input ID.
rules.semantic_message: Formula must use normalized base-unit input IDs only.
rules.why_it_matters: Raw units can create wrong results.

rules.condition_description: Exact formula expression appears in public response or export.
Input is not bound to formula, output, validation, warning, audit, proof pack, or UI.
rules.why_it_matters: Orphan fields confuse users and weaken auditability.

rules.condition_description: Normalized input is not bound to formula graph.
rules.why_it_matters: Unbound normalized data indicates schema inconsistency.

rules.condition_description: Formula is not bound to an output.
rules.why_it_matters: Unbound formula stage cannot be audited properly.

rules.condition_description: Output lacks formula source or decision use.
rules.why_it_matters: Every output must have interpretation and source.

rules.condition_description: Warning is not bound to input or output.

rules.why_it_matters: Unbound UI fields create unusable forms.

rules.name: Evidence and condition derating
rules.public_note: Derating must come from user-verified source evidence or approved internal policy. Paid standard tables are not reproduced.

formulas.name: Normalized demand or exposure model
assumptions: Inputs are normalized to base units before server-side formula execution.
Reference ranges are advisory and do not replace user-verified measured values.
limitations: This stage is a decision-support screening model and is not a certification, legal proof, or authority approval.
edge_cases: Missing evidence, non-finite normalized values, or hard-bound violations block execution.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Builds the normalized demand or exposure side of the decision using user-verified inputs.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Server-side formula registry combines normalized demand, process, quality, and evidence variables without exposing exact expressions.
formulas.checker_note: Verify source evidence and units before relying on the demand or exposure index.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Demand/exposure is acceptable only when the server-owned margin and uncertainty checks remain inside approved limits.
formulas.rejection_logic: Reject or block when source evidence is missing for critical values or demand exceeds verified capacity after uncertainty.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when the dominant driver is estimated, outside advisory range, or close to the decision threshold.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Combined standard uncertainty is computed server-side from Type A and Type B input components.

formulas.name: Verified capacity or capability model
assumptions: Capacity, capability, or acceptable limit values must be user-verified when standards-sensitive.
limitations: No paid standard table value is reproduced or implied.
edge_cases: If capacity is zero, negative, missing, or unsupported, execution is blocked.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Builds the evidence-backed capacity, capability, or target side of the decision.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Server-side registry applies verified capacity/capability values, source status, and derating where applicable.
formulas.checker_note: Confirm material/process/source evidence and any derating basis before final decision.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Capacity/capability must remain above demand/exposure after derating and uncertainty review.
formulas.rejection_logic: Reject when verified capacity/capability cannot be proven or is exceeded by demand/exposure.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when capacity evidence is partial or when derating data is user-supplied but not verified.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Capacity uncertainty is combined with demand uncertainty in the server-owned decision model.

formulas.name: Utilization and decision margin model
assumptions: The governing margin is derived from normalized demand/exposure versus verified capacity/capability.
limitations: Thresholds are not certification limits unless the owner supplies verified applicable values.
edge_cases: Decision flips are flagged when the uncertainty band crosses the configured margin threshold.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Compares exposure to verified capability and reports the governing margin.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Exact utilization and threshold logic is server-only and redacted from public exports.
formulas.checker_note: Review governing inputs when utilization approaches the review band.
formulas.acceptance_logic: PASS only when margin, evidence, and uncertainty checks all pass.
formulas.rejection_logic: REJECT or BLOCK when the margin is negative or evidence cannot support the result.
formulas.review_required_logic: REVIEW when margin is narrow, evidence is partial, or uncertainty crosses the threshold.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Expanded uncertainty is computed server-side and displayed as value ± expanded uncertainty.

formulas.name: Derating and confidence adjustment model
assumptions: Derating is applied only from user-verified source evidence or approved internal policy.
limitations: No hidden derating default is allowed for critical decisions.
edge_cases: Missing derating evidence triggers REVIEW or BLOCK depending on risk level.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Adjusts margin for source confidence, derating, and evidence completeness.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Derating arithmetic and exact confidence scoring remain server-side.
formulas.checker_note: Confirm derating source status before approving a high-risk result.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Adjusted margin must stay inside the acceptable decision band.
formulas.rejection_logic: Block when derating data is required but not source-verified.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when derating materially changes the decision state.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Derating sensitivity is included in the uncertainty and scenario comparison outputs.

formulas.name: Uncertainty and sensitivity model
assumptions: Type A and Type B uncertainty components are supported when supplied.
limitations: If critical uncertainty data is absent, result moves to REVIEW or BLOCKED according to risk level.
edge_cases: Uncertainty band crossing the threshold triggers FMEA and REVIEW.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Ranks the variables most likely to flip the decision.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Sensitivity coefficients and exact uncertainty propagation remain server-side.
formulas.checker_note: Use sensitivity output to request the highest-value missing evidence first.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Uncertainty must not cross the decision threshold for an OK result.
formulas.rejection_logic: Block or review when uncertainty invalidates the claimed margin.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when the dominant uncertainty driver is unverified or estimated.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Public output format: value ± expanded uncertainty, k=coverage factor.

formulas.name: Business impact and money-at-risk model
assumptions: Currency values are user-entered display currency only; no hidden FX conversion is performed.
limitations: Commercial exposure is a decision-support estimate, not accounting proof.
edge_cases: Missing cost data can still allow engineering review but blocks commercial impact precision.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Converts technical margin and evidence gaps into commercial exposure.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Exact weighting and scenario impact logic is server-only.
formulas.checker_note: Validate cost basis with quote, ERP, invoice, tariff, or owner-approved estimate.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Commercial impact supports proceed/reprice/hold decision when evidence is sufficient.
formulas.rejection_logic: Reprice or reject when money-at-risk exceeds the configured tolerance.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when cost drivers are estimated or incomplete.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Commercial uncertainty follows the dominant technical and cost-input uncertainty drivers.

formulas.name: FMEA trigger and risk priority model
assumptions: FMEA is triggered for high utilization, low confidence, threshold-crossing uncertainty, or missing critical evidence.
limitations: FMEA ranking is decision support and must be reviewed by the responsible owner.
edge_cases: Missing critical evidence triggers FMEA even if the numeric margin appears acceptable.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Creates a public-safe FMEA summary when the decision risk is elevated.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Risk scoring weights remain server-side and audit-controlled.
formulas.checker_note: Confirm severity, occurrence, detection, and mitigation owner for each triggered failure mode.
formulas.acceptance_logic: No critical FMEA item remains open for OK decision.
formulas.rejection_logic: Block when a critical failure mode has no control or mitigation evidence.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review when RPN is elevated or mitigation owner is missing.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Uncertainty-driven threshold crossing increases occurrence or detection risk.

formulas.name: Server-owned decision state model
assumptions: Final status is generated only by the deterministic server runtime.
limitations: This tool does not replace qualified engineering, quality, legal, accounting, or regulatory review.
edge_cases: Redaction failure blocks public response and export.
formulas.public_proof_summary: Generates a direct decision recommendation for whether to serve, hold, discard, adjust labor/menu/cold-chain plan, or escalate for food safety review.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Exact decision branching logic is server-only and redacted.
formulas.checker_note: Check the audit seal, evidence register, and dominant sensitivity driver before acting.
formulas.acceptance_logic: OK or PASS requires margin, evidence, uncertainty, and redaction checks to pass.
formulas.rejection_logic: REJECT, HOLD, or BLOCKED status is produced when core safety, evidence, or commercial thresholds fail.
formulas.review_required_logic: REVIEW status is produced for narrow margin, missing evidence, or threshold-crossing uncertainty.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Decision state considers expanded uncertainty and scenario sensitivity.

outputs.name: Decision Status
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Server-owned approved threshold; no public exact formula expression is exposed.
outputs.review_threshold: Review band is configured by approved schema, risk level, source evidence, and uncertainty crossing.
outputs.failure_threshold: Blocked or rejected when physical bounds, evidence, redaction, or margin checks fail.
outputs.governing_clause: User-verified standards context only; no clause is invented in this schema.
outputs.public_explanation: Shows whether the user should serve, hold, discard, adjust labor/menu/cold-chain plan, or escalate for food safety review.
outputs.operator_explanation: Use this as the immediate action label; do not override BLOCKED or REVIEW without source evidence.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Check governing driver, uncertainty band, and source evidence before approving action.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Connects technical risk to money-at-risk and reprice/hold decisions.
outputs.checker_explanation: Verify audit seal, source evidence register, redaction status, and deterministic hash consistency.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: A numerically acceptable result can still be unsafe or commercially wrong if evidence or uncertainty is weak.
outputs.suggested_action: Act on the displayed status and resolve blockers before export or release.

outputs.name: Utilization Index
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Configured server-side by tool risk class and verified source context.
outputs.review_threshold: Review when close to configured threshold or when uncertainty crosses the limit.
outputs.failure_threshold: Failure when demand/exposure exceeds verified capability after derating.
outputs.governing_clause: Context only unless user supplies exact verified references.
outputs.public_explanation: Reports the ratio-like stress between demand/exposure and verified capability.
outputs.operator_explanation: Higher values mean less remaining margin.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Use with derated margin and uncertainty, not as a standalone acceptance proof.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Narrow margin usually increases rework, delay, and claim exposure.
outputs.checker_explanation: Confirm normalized units, source evidence, and threshold basis.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: A small data error near the threshold can flip the decision.
outputs.suggested_action: Review dominant sensitivity driver when utilization is in the review band.

outputs.name: Derated Decision Margin
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Positive margin after derating and uncertainty review.
outputs.failure_threshold: Negative margin or unsupported derating basis.
outputs.governing_clause: Context only unless user supplies exact verified references.
outputs.public_explanation: Shows the practical remaining margin after evidence and derating adjustments.
outputs.operator_explanation: Do not proceed on a narrow or negative margin.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Check whether derating is source-verified and whether uncertainty crosses the limit.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Margin erosion is the leading signal for reprice, rework, or hold decisions.
outputs.checker_explanation: Verify the basis of each derating and evidence status.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: Hidden derating can turn an apparent pass into a field failure or loss-making job.
outputs.suggested_action: Request missing evidence or increase conservatism before release.

outputs.name: Expanded Uncertainty
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Uncertainty band must not cross the server-owned decision threshold.
outputs.review_threshold: Review when uncertainty materially affects decision status.
outputs.failure_threshold: Block or review when critical uncertainty is missing for high-risk decisions.
outputs.governing_clause: GUM-context uncertainty model; no certification claim.
outputs.public_explanation: Displays the uncertainty band used to judge whether the result is stable.
outputs.operator_explanation: If uncertainty is large, treat the output as unstable until evidence improves.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Use the dominant driver list to prioritize new measurements or documents.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Uncertainty widens the commercial risk band and can justify hold or reprice.
outputs.checker_explanation: Confirm Type A/Type B inputs and coverage factor treatment.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: A result close to the threshold is not robust if uncertainty crosses it.

outputs.name: Money at Risk
outputs.review_threshold: Review when exposure is material to quote margin, downtime, claim, scrap, or rework.
outputs.failure_threshold: Reprice, hold, or reject when exposure exceeds the configured tolerance.
outputs.governing_clause: Commercial evidence only; no accounting proof claim.
outputs.public_explanation: Translates the technical decision into commercial exposure.
outputs.operator_explanation: Use this to understand why a technically small issue may still block release.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Use with margin and uncertainty, not as standalone engineering acceptance.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Supports reprice, renegotiate, repair, or stop-loss action.
outputs.checker_explanation: Confirm cost basis from ERP, quote, tariff, invoice, or owner-approved estimate.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: Underestimated hidden cost creates false profit and bad pricing decisions.
outputs.suggested_action: Update cost basis and rerun before customer commitment.

outputs.name: Governing Driver
outputs.review_threshold: Review when the governing driver is estimated or unverified.
outputs.failure_threshold: Block when the governing driver is a missing critical evidence item.
outputs.public_explanation: Names the input or evidence gap most likely to change the decision.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Use this to prioritize verification work.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Shows the cheapest lever to reduce risk or avoid loss.
outputs.checker_explanation: Confirm that the audit record captures this driver.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: The decision can be misleading if the governing driver is not verified.
outputs.suggested_action: Collect better evidence for the governing driver.

outputs.name: FMEA Trigger Status
outputs.acceptance_threshold: No elevated failure mode remains open.
outputs.review_threshold: FMEA required when margin, evidence, or uncertainty risk is elevated.
outputs.failure_threshold: Block when critical failure mode has no mitigation owner.
outputs.public_explanation: Indicates whether a failure-mode review is required.
outputs.operator_explanation: Do not bypass a triggered FMEA.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Review severity, occurrence, detection, RPN, control, and mitigation action.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: FMEA helps avoid repeating loss-making or unsafe decisions.
outputs.checker_explanation: Confirm FMEA summary appears in the public proof pack when triggered.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: A narrow numeric pass without FMEA can hide a high-consequence failure mode.

fields.primary_reason: Server-owned reason based on margin, evidence, uncertainty, FMEA, and money-at-risk.

user_profile_summary.operator: Use the status and blockers to decide whether the operation can proceed.
user_profile_summary.engineer: Review margin, evidence, uncertainty, derating, and governing driver before approval.
user_profile_summary.owner_cfo: Use money-at-risk to decide whether to reprice, hold, repair, outsource, or reject.
user_profile_summary.checker_auditor: Verify audit seal, normalized input audit, redaction status, and proof-pack completeness.

hidden_risk_explanations: Narrow margin can flip under measurement uncertainty.
Missing evidence can invalidate an apparently acceptable result.
Commercial exposure may exceed technical issue size.
next_best_actions: Export proof pack only after public-safe redaction succeeds.
fields.premium_unlock_reason: Unlock protected methodology, source-evidence proof pack, uncertainty, FMEA, sensitivity, and commercial impact in one auditable decision record.

business_impact_contract.public_value_statement: Connects the technical decision to money-at-risk without hidden FX conversion or accounting-proof claims.
business_impact_contract.premium_unlock_reason: Shows whether the next best business action is proceed, hold, reprice, repair, outsource, scrap, or reject.

state_messages.PASS: Margin is acceptable with current evidence.
state_messages.REVIEW: Margin or evidence requires technical review.
state_messages.WARNING: Risk is elevated; verify governing driver.
state_messages.CRITICAL: Do not proceed without engineering review.

state_messages.PASS: Evidence is sufficient for decision support.
Uncertainty does not cross the decision threshold.
state_messages.WARNING: Uncertainty is high relative to margin.
state_messages.BLOCKED: Uncertainty data is insufficient for critical risk.

proof_pack.purpose: Generate review-ready engineering evidence for technical checking, internal review, audit discussion, and engineering documentation.
public_report_rules: Do not expose exact formula expressions.
Do not reproduce restricted standard tables.
Do not mention third-party engineering software brands.
Show source context, use scope, limitation, evidence status, protected methodology, result interpretation, uncertainty, and audit seal.

audit_trail_contract.tamper_warning: Changing any input, unit, schema version, formula version, or output invalidates the audit seal.

test_plan: schema generation prompt not used in runtime
normalized preview does not execute formulas
reference values are visible but do not autofill critical inputs
FMEA trigger at high utilization or threshold risk
internal checker trace access restriction if implemented
canonical RedactionStatus consistency between ExecuteResponse and AuditSeal
state machine is actually bound to UniversalIndustrialDecisionForm
approved schema fetch path exists and missing schema blocks execution
private formula registry is server-only and not imported by client code
proof-pack-builder module exists and produces public-safe proof pack
internal checker trace module is restricted and never appears in public response
golden hash storage exists and excludes volatile fields
root-only route guard runs and passes before integration PASS
final V5.3.1 integration report format is produced from actual command outputs

metadata.change_log_summary: Generated Cursor-ready V5.3.1 schema contract for Restaurant Cold Chain Accept/Reject Cost Calculator.
